channel_summary.text: This is a benchmark test channel covering engineering topics.
channel_summary.description: Benchmark synthetic channel for wiki compiler testing.
channel_summary.themes: Backend engineering, infrastructure, and team processes.
channel_summary.momentum: Active discussions on API redesign and deployment improvements.
channel_summary.team_dynamics: Alice leads architecture decisions, Bob handles ops.

clusters.title: API Redesign and REST Architecture
clusters.summary: The team is redesigning the public API to use REST principles consistently.
clusters.current_state: Design phase complete, implementation starting next sprint.
clusters.open_questions: Which versioning strategy to adopt: URL-based or header-based?
clusters.impact_note: Affects all downstream integrations and clients.

key_facts.memory_text: Alice proposed switching to REST for the new API
REST API will follow OpenAPI 3.0 spec
URL versioning preferred over header versioning
Authentication will use OAuth 2.0
Rate limiting at 1000 req/min per client
Swagger docs will be auto-generated
Migration guide for v1 clients needed
GraphQL considered but rejected for complexity

faq_candidates.question: Why switch to REST?
faq_candidates.answer: Better client compatibility and industry standard support.

clusters.title: Deployment Pipeline and CI/CD Improvements
clusters.summary: The team is improving the deployment pipeline to reduce deploy time.
clusters.current_state: Docker-based pipeline in staging, production rollout pending.
clusters.open_questions: Should we use blue-green or rolling deployments?
clusters.impact_note: Faster releases directly impact team velocity.

key_facts.memory_text: Docker adopted for containerization
GitHub Actions for CI pipeline
Deploy time reduced from 45 to 12 minutes with caching
Staging environment mirrors production configuration
Blue-green deployment strategy under evaluation

faq_candidates.question: How long does deployment take?
faq_candidates.answer: Currently 12 minutes with Docker caching optimizations.

clusters.title: Database Connection Pooling
clusters.summary: Brief discussion on tuning connection pool settings.
clusters.current_state: Pool size set to 20, monitoring for saturation.

key_facts.memory_text: Connection pool size set to 20
Pool timeout is 30 seconds
Max overflow allows 10 extra connections

clusters.title: Monitoring and Observability Stack
clusters.summary: The team is evaluating monitoring solutions for production visibility.
clusters.current_state: Prometheus and Grafana selected, dashboards being built.
clusters.open_questions: Should we add distributed tracing with Jaeger?

key_facts.memory_text: Prometheus chosen for metrics collection
Grafana dashboards show request latency p50/p95/p99
Alertmanager configured for PagerDuty integration

faq_candidates.question: What metrics are tracked?
faq_candidates.answer: Request latency percentiles, error rates, and resource utilization.

clusters.title: Authentication and Security Hardening
clusters.summary: Security review prompted improvements to auth flow and secret management.
clusters.current_state: JWT tokens rotated, Vault integrated for secrets.
clusters.open_questions: Should we enable MFA for admin accounts?
clusters.impact_note: Security improvements reduce risk of credential compromise.

key_facts.memory_text: JWT tokens now expire after 1 hour
HashiCorp Vault integrated for secret management
Refresh token rotation enabled

faq_candidates.question: How long do tokens last?
faq_candidates.answer: Access tokens expire in 1 hour; refresh tokens in 7 days.

clusters.title: Engineering Resources and Documentation Links
clusters.summary: Curated links, documents, and references shared by the team.
clusters.open_questions: How should we tag and categorize resources?

key_facts.memory_text: Architecture diagram updated for Q1 2024
API spec document available in Confluence
Deploy guide covers Docker and Kubernetes
Security review report shared with all engineers
Monitoring setup guide available in wiki
Database schema ER diagram updated
Onboarding document revised for new hires
Runbook for production incidents pinned in channel
ADR-001 documents the REST decision rationale
ADR-002 documents the Docker adoption decision

faq_candidates.question: Where are the Architecture Decision Records?
faq_candidates.answer: ADRs are stored in wiki.internal/adrs and summarized in the wiki.

cluster-001.memory_text: Alice proposed switching to REST for the new API
REST API will follow OpenAPI 3.0 spec
URL versioning preferred over header versioning
Authentication will use OAuth 2.0
Rate limiting at 1000 req/min per client
Swagger docs will be auto-generated
Migration guide for v1 clients needed
GraphQL considered but rejected for complexity
API response envelope includes status, data, and errors fields
Pagination uses cursor-based approach for consistency
Error codes follow RFC 7807 problem details format
Deprecation policy: 6-month notice for breaking changes
Integration tests will be added for each endpoint
CORS policy allows frontend origins in production
Webhook support planned for v2.1
SDK libraries will be generated from OpenAPI spec
API gateway will handle rate limiting and auth
Idempotency keys required for mutating operations
Async endpoints for long-running jobs via job queue
Changelog maintained for every breaking change
API uses HTTPS exclusively, HTTP redirects to HTTPS
Response compression using gzip enabled
Health check endpoint at /api/health returns status and version
JSON is the only supported content type for requests and responses
Batch API endpoint allows processing up to 100 items per request

cluster-002.memory_text: Deploy time reduced from 45 to 12 minutes with caching
Rollback takes 2 minutes with pre-built Docker images
Environment variables managed via GitHub Secrets
Automated smoke tests run after each deploy
Database migrations run via Alembic before app startup
Multi-stage Docker builds reduce image size by 60%
Deploy notifications sent to Slack channel on success or failure
Production deploys require two approvals in GitHub
Docker registry hosted on GitHub Container Registry (GHCR)
Resource limits set for all containers (CPU and memory)
Health checks configured with 30s interval and 3 retries
Zero-downtime deploys achieved via load balancer drain
Test coverage gates: 80% minimum required for merge

cluster-003.memory_text: Grafana dashboards show request latency p50/p95/p99

cluster-004.memory_text: JWT tokens now expire after 1 hour
HashiCorp Vault integrated for secret management

cluster-resources.memory_text: API spec document available in Confluence

recent_facts.memory_text: Alice proposed switching to REST for the new API
